message:
test(mcp-conformance): wa.mission_*/wa.tx_* envelope golden roundtrip harness + placeholders (ft-qiba0)

Nine transactional MCP tools (wa.mission_state, wa.mission_explain,
wa.mission_pause, wa.mission_resume, wa.mission_abort, wa.tx_plan,
wa.tx_show, wa.tx_run, wa.tx_rollback) had manifest-freeze coverage
via mcp_conformance_manifest.rs (schema gate, additionalProperties=false)
but no committed request→response envelope roundtrip. A refactor that
renames a response field in any of them passes manifest conformance
while silently breaking every mission/tx client.

Mirrors the scope-fencing pattern from ft-4o5mb (wa.search family):
  1. TestHarness with a tempdir workspace + framework MCP server.
  2. spawn_client() → call_tool() with a deterministic minimal
     Mission / TxPlan / Assignment / CandidateAction fixture chain.
  3. assert_matches_golden(name, capture) roundtrips the
     ToolGoldenCapture {tool, input_schema, success_envelope,
     invalid_args_envelope} through canonical pretty JSON, compares
     against tests/golden_robot_envelope/<name>.json, and on drift
     writes <name>.actual.json next to the expected file for diff
     review.

UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 regenerates the goldens in-place. The committed
tests/golden_robot_envelope/wa_{mission_*,tx_*}.json files are empty
{} placeholders to reserve the file paths; on first real run the
harness will either populate them (with UPDATE_GOLDEN=1) or panic
with an explicit regenerate-with-UPDATE_GOLDEN hint. Committing the
placeholders (vs leaving them missing) keeps the dir tracked and
makes the regenerate flow a one-command operation.

Requires features = ["mcp", "asupersync-runtime"]; gated with
#![cfg(feature = "mcp")].

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
